name: New Beginnings Community Consulting & Restoration
description_short: Detroit-based credit restoration company founded by Tierra Jae helping clients dispute negative items, manage bankruptcy, and expunge criminal records.
description_long:
New Beginnings Community Consulting & Restoration (NBCCR) is a credit repair and social services company founded by serial entrepreneur Tierra Jae from Detroit, Michigan. The company was established after Tierra personally rebuilt her own credit and recognized the need to help others navigate similar financial and legal challenges. NBCCR operates as a full-service restoration firm addressing multiple barriers to financial stability that extend beyond traditional credit repair.

The company offers credit restoration services, bankruptcy assistance, criminal record expungements, and felon-friendly resources. According to the founder's interview, NBCCR has helped over 300 clients across the country. The company has also developed proprietary products, including The Credit Calendar, described as the first Black-owned credit management planners, suggesting a focus on educational tools alongside restoration services.

NBCCR distinguishes itself through a holistic approach to financial restoration that combines credit repair with support for individuals facing collateral consequences of criminal records and bankruptcy. Tierra Jae's background as a woman founder from Detroit and her emphasis on serving underserved populations (particularly those with criminal records) sets the company apart from traditional credit repair competitors. The company has received media recognition and industry accolades, including magazine features.

The primary limitation evident from available information is the lack of detailed service descriptions, pricing transparency, or specific dispute methodologies on the provided website excerpt. The company profile relies heavily on a single medium-length founder interview rather than comprehensive service documentation. No information is available regarding company size, response times, success rates, or regulatory compliance details that would be relevant for consumer evaluation.

In the broader ecosystem of credit repair services, consumers have multiple paths to improving their credit. Professional credit repair companies can dispute inaccurate items with all three bureaus, while credit monitoring services provide ongoing alerts about changes to your reports. For those building credit from scratch, secured credit cards and credit builder loans offer structured approaches. Consumers dealing with overwhelming debt may benefit from debt consolidation loans to simplify payments, or credit counseling through nonprofit agencies for personalized budgeting guidance. Consumers who successfully repair their credit often find better rates on installment loans, secured credit cards, and other financial products.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Founded by woman entrepreneur with personal credit-building experience
Serves over 300 clients across the country with documented track record
Offers integrated services including criminal record expungement and felon-friendly resources beyond credit repair alone
Developed proprietary educational product (The Credit Calendar) as first Black-owned credit management planner
Founder has received industry recognition and magazine features demonstrating credibility
Addresses barriers for underserved populations including individuals with criminal histories
cons: No pricing information, fee structure, or service costs disclosed in available materials
Limited documentation of specific dispute processes, success rates, or measurable outcomes per client
Website content is primarily a founder interview rather than comprehensive service documentation
No information about company size, team credentials, or certifications (NACARS, Better Business Bureau status)
Lacks transparency on timeline expectations, guarantee policies, or specific credit report dispute methodology
best_for: Individuals with both credit damage and criminal record concerns seeking integrated solutions
Clients seeking culturally-aligned credit services from a Black-owned business
People exploring bankruptcy options who need combined legal and credit guidance
Those re-entering the workforce after incarceration looking for comprehensive financial restoration
diagnosis: NBCCR is best for individuals requiring integrated credit repair combined with criminal record or bankruptcy support, particularly those seeking a minority-owned business with founder-led guidance. The main caveat is limited publicly available information about service specifics, pricing, success metrics, and regulatory standing—interested consumers should request detailed service documentation and fee schedules directly from the company before engaging.
services: The Credit Calendar (proprietary credit planning tool)
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
address: 17801 W Seven Mile Rd, Detroit, MI 48235
